title: NOC 41100 – Judges | GTR Immigration
description: Judges under NOC 41100 are senior legal professionals who preside over courts, interpret laws, and ensure justice is administered fairly across Canada. Classified as TEER 1 (Professional Occupation), this role represents one of the highest levels within Canada’s legal system. Judicial positions exist within provincial, territorial, and federal courts and play a fundamental role in maintaining Canada’s rule of law.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category4 – Occupations in education, law, and social, community, and government servicesTEER1 – Occupations usually require a university degreeMajor Group41 – Professional occupations in lawSub-Major Group411 – Professional occupations in lawMinor Group4110 – Judges, lawyers and Quebec notariesNOC Code41100

duties: Judges perform some or all of the following duties:
Determine guilt, innocence, or civil liability
Supervise other judges and court officers
employment_requirements: Extensive experience as a lawyer or professor of law
Continuous membership in good standing with a provincial or territorial law society
Judicial appointment by federal or provincial cabinet
Senior judicial roles (e.g., Chief Justice) require prior judicial experience
Membership in a provincial law society such as the Law Society of Ontario is mandatory prior to judicial consideration.
Complete National Committee on Accreditation (NCA) requirements
Be called to the bar in a Canadian province
salary_prospects: Annual earnings typically range from $254,600 to $383,400, depending on jurisdiction and court level.
job_outlook: Appointments remain highly competitive and merit-based.
labour_market_demand: Judges are appointed to serve in:
Judicial appointments are made by federal or provincial cabinets depending on the level of court. Opportunities are concentrated in provinces such as Ontario, Quebec, British Columbia, and Alberta.
Strong judicial independence and job security
Leadership role within Canada’s justice system
However, judicial appointments in Canada require Canadian legal credentials and membership in a provincial or territorial law society.
pathways: While NOC 41100 is a high-skilled TEER 1 occupation, becoming a Judge in Canada requires:
Foreign legal professionals seeking immigration may first consider:
Express Entry under legal professional categories
Licensing as a lawyer before judicial eligibility
Long-term pathway mapping toward judicial eligibility
Contact GTR Immigration to evaluate your immigration pathway to Canada’s legal profession.
